content: Global copper markets shifted to a 300,000-tonne deficit in 2024 as mine disruptions in Panama (Cobre Panama closure) and Chile (grade decline) constrained supply. China's smelter treatment charges fell to historic lows ($10/tonne vs $80-100 long-term average), indicating concentrate scarcity. LME copper prices averaged $8,500/tonne in Q4 2024, up 15% year-over-year.
